articles.title: Two Children Die in House Fire
articles.summary: A house fire happened in Wolverhampton on a Friday evening. Firefighters and police came to help. Two young children were inside the house. Firefighters saved them, but sadly, the children could not live. They died at the scene. Two other children and a woman were already outside before the firefighters arrived. They were checked by doctors and were okay. The police are now trying to find out what caused the fire. The house was badly damaged. Everyone is very sad about what happened.

articles.title: Fight on TV show 'I'm a Celebrity'
articles.summary: On the final night of the TV show 'I'm a Celebrity... Get Me Out of Here!', there was a big argument. The show is set in a jungle in South Africa, where famous people live together and do challenges. In this special version, past contestants came back. During a challenge, footballer Jimmy Bullard quit, which made actor Adam Thomas very angry. He shouted at Jimmy using bad words. The hosts Ant and Dec had to raise their voices to stop the fight. Some other stars, like Sinitta and Gemma Collins, walked away. In the end, Adam Thomas won the show and became the 'king of the jungle'. Later, Adam said he was proud of staying true to himself. The producers didn't show all of his angry outburst because it was too strong. Other contestants said Adam looked like he might attack Jimmy. Jimmy called Adam's reaction silly and said he quit because he wanted to go home.

articles.title: US Threatens Spain Over NATO
articles.summary: European leaders met in Cyprus to discuss the EU budget, but a new problem came up. A leaked email from the US Pentagon said the US might punish Spain because Spain did not support the US-Israel war against Iran. The email suggested the US could try to kick Spain out of NATO. But NATO rules do not allow expelling a member. Other European leaders like the Dutch prime minister and a German official defended Spain. They said Spain is a full NATO member and should stay that way. The Italian prime minister also criticized the US for tensions with Spain. The US president, Donald Trump, is angry with Spain and other countries like the UK for not fully supporting the war. This is causing problems between the US and its European allies.

articles.title: Flood Warning System in the Everest Region is Broken
articles.summary: A flood warning system in the Everest region of Nepal is broken. The system was built to warn people about dangerous floods from a lake called Imja. The lake is high up in the mountains, over 5,000 meters above sea level. After the lake was drained in 2016, no one fixed the warning system. Now, the towers that have sirens are rusty and some batteries have been stolen. The system also sends alerts to mobile phones, but that part is not working well. Many people live near the lake, and if it floods, they could be in danger. Scientists say that because of global warming, glaciers are melting faster and lakes are getting bigger. This makes floods more likely. Villagers are worried because there have been five floods in the last 50 years. They want the government to fix the system to keep everyone safe.

articles.title: Justice Department Ends Investigation of Fed Chair
articles.summary: The Justice Department has stopped looking into whether Jerome Powell, the leader of the Federal Reserve, lied about repairs at his office. Instead, another group will check on the costs. This news is important because it means a new person, Kevin Warsh, might become the next Fed chair. The White House wants the Senate to approve him quickly. Also, a court said President Trump cannot stop people from asking for asylum at the southern border. The court said the law allows people to seek safety. The government can ask a higher court to change this decision. Lastly, the FDA is going to speed up reviews of three new medicines for mental health conditions.

articles.title: Soldier Gets in Trouble for Betting on Secret Raid
articles.summary: A U.S. soldier helped plan a secret mission to catch the leader of Venezuela, President Nicolas Maduro. After the mission, the soldier made bets on a website called Polymarket and won over $400,000. The problem is that he used secret information to make those bets. That is called insider trading, and it is against the law. Now the soldier is in trouble and may have to go to court. People are worried that this kind of betting on important events needs stricter rules. The soldier’s arrest shows that using secret information to make money on prediction markets is not allowed.
